title: sorbus aucuparia var. praemorsa
intro: Sorbus aucuparia var. praemorsa, also known as the rowan tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree with clusters of white or pink flowers and red berries. The bark and leaves of the rowan tree have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The berries of the rowan tree have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to their high content of anthocyanins and other antioxidants. The rowan tree can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies and natural health products.
